name: CARAT - CARbon Agroforestry Tool
description: The CARbon Agroforestry Tool “CARAT” is an online tool developed by the Soil Service of Belgium (BDB), UGent and ILVO. While the original version was made using the software program R combined with the Shiny package for the interface, the latest version launched is made in Python. The primary goal of the CARAT tool is to support farmers, researchers and/or policy actors in quantifying carbon stocks of a specific AF field in temperate climatic conditions, and to predict how these stocks evolve over a period of 30 years. At present, the tool is aimed at simulating AF systems that consist of high pruned solitary trees. Choice between 20 different tree species is made possible. The user is provided with a field-scale map showing heterogeneities in SOC as a result of tree presence, as well as an estimation of above- and belowground carbon stocks at field level and their evolution with time.
developers: Tom Coussement, Eveline Baens, Dries de Bièvre, Annemie Elsen, Thomas Vanneste, Kris Verheyen, Bert Reubens, Paul Pardon, Liselot De Praetere, Sarah Carton
organizations: Soil Service of Belgium (BDB), UGent Forest and Nature lab (Fornalab), Flanders research institute for agriculture, fisheries and food (ILVO)
output_data_formats: Static content (e.g. reports on website pages, tables and graphs in the tool's interface, PDF files)
access_barrier: No, direct download/access through a web link
access_procedure: request access via the zenodo record of the tool: https://zenodo.org/records/11503743
documentation_theoretical_background_lis: Vanneste, T., De Praetere, L., Pardon, P. et al. (2025) CARAT: an innovative tool for quantifying carbon sequestration in agroforestry systems. Agroforest Syst 99, 68. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10457-025-01162-3
feedback_tool_info: a new demovideo (for the python version) will be launched soon
